title: veronica serpyllifolia var. repens
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia var. repens, also known as creeping speedwell, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb with small blue or purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of creeping speedwell include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. Creeping speedwell can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, eczema, and respiratory problems.
